usability_preview.1. Which of the two tools did you find e: SecureMyEmail. Account creation was lengthy but easier to understand other things/process.
usability_preview.2. Did either tool feel more secure or t: Both. SecureMyEmail's visualization is better so better trustworthy.
usability_preview.3. What was the most frustrating part of: Finding or managing the encryption key.
usability_preview.4. What would stop you from using encryp: Gmail is easier to use. Gmail is accessible to everyone.
usability_preview.5. How steep was the learning curve for: SecureMyEmail. Easier to visualize and use.
usability_preview.6. If you could improve one thing in eac:
ProtonMail: Better visualization of encryption process. 
SecureMyEmail: Account creation process. But those are necessary, so if avoided maybe arise some issues if some are deleted. But as one time use, its manageable.
usability_preview.7. Would you recommend any of these tool: SecureMyEmail. I felt comfortable using this.
usability_preview.8. In what personal or professional cont: Sensitive personal Image sharing. Sensitive data for work.

usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: If i imagine myself at home, and i need to open Proton Mail, i would open it because it is private than gamail. 1. I want to maintain privacy.  2. As per requirement, i dont need it right now. But if i need to secure any data than i would prefer protonmail than gmail, because it is providing suggestions about secure my data. 3. It sould be very much suitable for professional life because i wouldnt want to insecure my data.
usability_preview.9. Did anything confuse or surprise you?: 1. Authenticator app download 2. If connection slow, 10 mins delay between another code. 3. Authenticator guide
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: Layout overwhelmed for mail usage. If I am 35/40 but want to send a secure mail, if i enter the layout it is very overwhelmed, Many options are available altogether. Should be more focused on the core part and have other features on different sections.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple:
Firstly, if the protonMail user, the encryption is properly visible. They are using a separate space for encryption. But Non Protonmail user, its brain straining. There is a 7 minutes reading. there are things to enable or disable. If i dont know what to enable or disable i would be lost. 
If i had 0 idea, i would be very lost. What is PGP or what would happen if i do this or that. 
If i have time then it is okay, other than that it would be stressful. Non protonMail, its easy but not that easy.
usability_preview.19. Did anything confuse or surprise you:
Confusing was, PGP option selection. Their encryption was like if i attach public key, it would be more secure but the explanation was not enough. Its there but its not there. I am confused about the encryption. I am understanding that it is encrypted, but i dont understand how the concept of public key is being handled there. Like the public key is there but i am not sure  what it is doing.

Surprising: Password Hint.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in:
PGP concept they are using. Options are clear but the options should have a reading what the options are doing or what they are doing. Could be more visual rather than readings. 
Those who are not tech enthusiast, they would be more benefitted for a visualization rather than readings. 
If it is a business situation, reading the process is not optimal. It would be more benificial with a chart or soemthing.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: I didn't need to think that much. The email was clearly indicated if it was encrypted or not. Quite easier.
usability_preview.29. Did anything confuse or surprise you: Phishing report is pleasantly surprising. The mail is encrypted but not signed thats very convenient.
usability_preview.30. What would you improve or change in: This section was very well structured and not overwhelming.
usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: SecureMyEmail need installing an external party. Too much work for sending important information. If its not toptier sceintific or sensitive info then i wouldnt choose this. This is too precise for new user. A new user will be startled by the UI/non description.
usability_preview.9. Did anything confuse or surprise you?: The advance section. I wanted more explanation for this section. It should be more interactive and explanatory in visual or writing. If someone has no idea, they would be very discouraged.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: Easy to navigate. I have not sent mail yet. Kinda confusing to understand whats going on on the whole thing. Need some integrations for general users for explanations.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: Secure contacts.I sent someone a mail but he is not in secure contact. There should be explanations about it how it works.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in: Security contact. If i add someone then what benefits i would get.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: It was easy. Compact for use. Those which are encrypted was being saved in a specific folder. Navigation was easy.
usability_preview.30. What would you improve or change in: Regular mail navigation so its not hard rather structured.
usability_preview.1. Which of the two tools did you find e:
For navigation SecureMyEmail. 
For understanding what and how i am doing ProtonMail.
usability_preview.2. Did either tool feel more secure or t: ProtonMail. I don't know why but it made me feel that way. Maybe because it was clearly understood. As i used Proton earlier i could use SecureMyEmail. But if i had not i would be lost in SecureMyEmail.
usability_preview.3. What was the most frustrating part of:
Gmail is visaully very interactive. If some is non-tech person uses these they would be confused. 
ProtonMail overwhelming. SecureMyEmail is very concise so nontech wouldnt understand.
usability_preview.4. What would stop you from using encryp: Firstly, i dont need it right now. Many wont do it due to understanding. No one would want to read for 15 mins. As for SecureMyEmail, i wont actually want an external entity to permit administrative use.
usability_preview.5. How steep was the learning curve for: ProtonMail learning has a good learning curve. But securemy email would be more easier to navigate but it would be so because i already used proton earlier than secure.
usability_preview.6. If you could improve one thing in eac:
ProtonMail interface because it is overwhelming, it could be more visual and interactive. 
SecureMyEmail representiona/explanatory part could be improved so user can understand what or how he is doing the encryption.
usability_preview.7. Would you recommend any of these tool: I would recommend proton to new comers. If someone already has some knowledge, i would recommend secure my email.
usability_preview.8. In what personal or professional cont: If i have a social security card or driving licsence, or something to send embassy then i would use these. We dont need it in general life. Sending information to higher authorities which i dont want anyone else to know. I would also use encryption for research.
usability_preview.9. Any final thoughts or feedback about: What i observed that they recognize the server. I read books , i use different emails for reading books. The encrypted mail server should be server compromised. The data shouldnt pass through any compromised network or server.

usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: Clear goal was what i had to do. The encryption key was not clearly present. If it was not included in the task then it would be hard enough to find encryption features. It was not highlighted enough.
usability_preview.9. Did anything confuse or surprise you?: Email encryption is a core feature but why user need it, it is not clearly mentioned. There is no walkthrough for email encryption. It is beneficial for them who knows it and dont need onboarding. There are no highlights of features.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: User feedback  should be more. Recovery was not clearly explained.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: Straightforward task, didn't understand if there were any levels of security.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: As a reciever, i thought both mail are same.
usability_preview.29. Did anything confuse or surprise you: Blue and black lock. The site assumed i know end-to-end encryption.
usability_preview.30. What would you improve or change in: User friendly label terms for end-to-end encryption process. Lock tooltip.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: Normal terms were used. Moderate knowlegable poeple would understand.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: Over straightforward, it was easy and regular.
usability_preview.1. Which of the two tools did you find e: SecureMyEmail. User friendly, didnt use hard terms. Moderate knowledgable persons would be able to use easily. Proton Mail is for advance people.
usability_preview.2. Did either tool feel more secure or t: The first one felt more secure as the complexity of that site is complex so mental biasness made me feel it was more secure.
usability_preview.3. What was the most frustrating part of: Learning curve, felt awkward. The informations was not present. I had to open a doc to udnerstand.
usability_preview.4. What would stop you from using encryp: Don't need sending any encrypted emails.
usability_preview.5. How steep was the learning curve for:
Easier to adopt: SecureMyEmail. 
The protonMails learning curve was steep.
usability_preview.6. If you could improve one thing in eac: The protonMails learning curve was steep. Making ProtonMail user friendly. Second one is very straightforward, so it made me feel it was too straight to be useful.
usability_preview.7. Would you recommend any of these tool: SecureMyMail. It was easier to use.
usability_preview.8. In what personal or professional cont: If i use any shared PC.

usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: Authenticator app usage , didnt know about it. 2FA faced issue. Used protonMail in past but didnt use it in depth.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: Authenticator app should be in built something. Authentication shouldn't depend on third party app.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: As i used before, proton to proton handled by default. I didnt add any extra layer. But when sending to non protonmail, i attached public key and password for that.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in: NonProton mail should be also handled as proton mails encryption handled by default if it could.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: Non proton mail mails was hardly identified as secured. The lock was a indiication of secure mail but aside that there was not indication of security. After going to message details it is shown if it is end to end encrypted But id idnt see it the first time.
usability_preview.29. Did anything confuse or surprise you: I was confused at first about to identify which are endto end . But after going to message details i had clarity which one were end to end encrypted.
usability_preview.30. What would you improve or change in: Indications of end to end encrypted mails hsould be better. Logo or sidenotes.  Clear indication visualtization.
usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: First of all it is unfamiliar to me. First time use. I was wondering how this work. What are the keys working. I dont know what the keys are doing here.
usability_preview.9. Did anything confuse or surprise you?: Nothing that much. Key generation was a bit confusing.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: Navigation of encryption key might be improved.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: Forwarding restrictions. Other than that it was simple. I didnt understand the mail connect first.
usability_preview.30. What would you improve or change in: Forwarding message restriction should have been available. One time view should be introduced.
usability_preview.1. Which of the two tools did you find e: ProtonMail. Because i used it realier and i was familiar. But secureMyEmail seemed faster. So i would prefer secureMyEmail more in case of speed.
usability_preview.2. Did either tool feel more secure or t: Both. Encryption keys, passoword, expirations times made me feel that way.
usability_preview.4. What would stop you from using encryp: Loading or sending of emails. I just used to use them for very secured mails. But for regular use i wouldnt prefer this.
usability_preview.5. How steep was the learning curve for:
ProtonMail: Didnt need to use anything extra. I used it earlier so the learning curve. SecureMyEmail: Steep learning curve. Had some issue to catch first time. 
As secureMyEmail handles a layer above Gmail so it would be easier to adopt for regular use(If i have paid plans.)
usability_preview.6. If you could improve one thing in eac:
ProtonMail: Should be more faster. 
SecureMyEmail: If it was integrated in gmail then it would be more preferable. Managing another application is not suitable.
usability_preview.7. Would you recommend any of these tool: I would recommend to send secure mails or sensitive data. Because there are password protections and expiration time.
usability_preview.8. In what personal or professional cont: When i am using a shared mail then i would must use these encryption services.

usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: I had to open account. It was easy. I inputed the fields and it was done. Then i added recovery mail and number. I didnt know about the authenticator app. But when i got to know about that it was very easy. After installing the app i scanned the qr code and it was done. The encryption key was clearly present in the dashboard. There was a clearly presented export option to export the public key.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: If someone is not familiar with the authenticator app, they wouldnt understand how to set it up.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: First of all, i went to inbox and then new message. I input the fields and sent. For the second one i attached a pdf and sent. As i sent it to a protonmail user i didnt understand if it was being end to end encrypted. For the nonprotonmail user it was displayed that it was being end to end encrypted. So, i easily understood that it was being end to end encrypted.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: First task was to read an encrypted message. It was easy just putting the password. The mail received from the protonmail, there was a blue lock on the left of the mail which said the mail was end to end encrypted. As for the non protonmail, there was a blue lock and a black lock. I assumed the blue lock was the encrypted message and i continued the conversation with that email.
usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet:
Firstly, i didnt know how to setup the account. There were no signup button. After exploring more i got to know that i needed to install the software to open a account. 
They needed a passphrase during account setup. I didnt find the  Security key regenration option easily. But the process was easy.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: For Signup , it should be clearly mentioned that the user has to install the software  to create an account.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: It was easy to send encrypted message. I had to attach an attachment then. I did successfully send to the securemyemail connected mail but couldnt send to the normal gmail. I could add additional security like expiration and password easily to the normal gmail but not the mail connected to securemyemail.
usability_preview.19. Did anything confuse or surprise you: When i tried to send attchment, i was redirected to draft continuously. I couldnt add additional feature for a mail. I dont know why it didnt let me add them.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in: Why the email is not being sent or why i cant add additional security it should be clarified.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: I had to open emails. One was encrypted and one was not. It was easy to identify because there was a lock button to indicate the encryption. The encrypted mails were organized in a different folder. I couldnt move any non encrypted mail tot hat folder. I continued the conversation for encrypted mail and it was encrypted through the process.
usability_preview.1. Which of the two tools did you find e: Account setup, encryption key access of Proton was easy. The email sending and receiving was easier in securemyemail. Also securemyemail organizes the encrypted emails to a different folder which is very user friendly.
usability_preview.2. Did either tool feel more secure or t: Both. Securemyemail was better. I was setting passwords, expirations in both of them. SecureMyEmail was not letting me add some features to some emails that was not clear to me.
usability_preview.3. What was the most frustrating part of: Authenticator app. Mail was going to draft in SecureMyEmail(Maybe because of network issue).
usability_preview.4. What would stop you from using encryp: I would prefer to use it regularly.
usability_preview.5. How steep was the learning curve for: ProtonMail was easier to learn. Both can be adopted. But i liked the UI of Proton more. But i would like securemyemail because i dont need to create any additional mail to handle.
usability_preview.7. Would you recommend any of these tool: SecureMyEmail. For proton , i need to create another email. But in securemyemail i can just use my existing email for additional security.

usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: The account setup was easy. They asked to upgrade the plan continuously that was irritating. 2 step verification was easy but it was not found so easily. Encryption key found was easy. The encryption key is mentioned as fingerprint which could be a confusion for nontech persons. I exported the key but dont know where it is stored.
usability_preview.9. Did anything confuse or surprise you?: They didnt have any guided tour.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: Promotion. It should appear one or two times. It should be displayed in sidebar. it shouldnt pop up continuosly. I would include a guided tour for the user. The encryption key should be more available or accessible.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: There was not clear understanding if the email was being encrypted or not. There was a text sent with proton mail securely which could be edited. I just had to believe blindly that it was end to end encrypted.
usability_preview.19. Did anything confuse or surprise you: Sent with proton Mail. External encryption is set. But i am not sure about the internal encryption. If the internal encryption is present then whats the use of external encryption. The password set for protonmail user i dont know if i need to do it.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in: Non proton mail input should be alerted or enforced with a highlight that user can send the mail securely. The option is present but it should be highlighted for non proton mail user. There is a redundant checkbox if i am sending this email to a non protonmail user.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: Every mail had a lock button. I didnt know how to identify. There was a shield tickmark which let me believe it was meant for encryption so i got misleaded by that.
usability_preview.29. Did anything confuse or surprise you: Every mail had lock sign. Shield tickmark. Password protected mails seemed encrypted(Sure) but others i was not sure if they were encrypted or not.
usability_preview.30. What would you improve or change in: There should be a clear indication of encryption. There should be mentioned that proton to proton is automatically encrypted.
usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: Nothing that much. Everything was labeled what it was for.
usability_preview.9. Did anything confuse or surprise you?: Why should i install an application. Why cant i access it at browser directly. I didnt know the wrods like SMIEE, PGP etc. They mentioned the key was downlaoded in my pc and stored in the server. But i didnt know where it was stored. It was there somewhere like a blind box.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: Account setup should be more easy using directly Oauth. The passphrase should be at account setup. Not directly after account creation. This should be browser based completely without use of a software. Ui could be enhanced.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: The encrypt and signature is enabled. That means the email is going through secure channel. It asked for passphrase for sending email. Non securemyemail user sending could be attached expiration. But securemyemail user it couldnt be added.
usability_preview.19. Did anything confuse or surprise you: Surprising part was when i was sending mail to existing user, it asked for passphrase, i thought my previous passphrase wouldnt be used here as i regenerated it. It was confusing. At one i needed the previous, at one i needed the current.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in: Mail compose section: Writing section UI. Encryption buttons should be dropdown with expiration/password. It should also work for existing users of securemyemail. Non existing user, after clcking send button the expiration date set is being displayed. But there should be option to add expiration before sending the email.
usability_preview.29. Did anything confuse or surprise you: One mail mentioned view secure message. But there was nothing after i opened the mail.
usability_preview.30. What would you improve or change in: I am putting two different passphrase(One i set initially and then other regenerated later). Lables have a icon
usability_preview.1. Which of the two tools did you find e: ProtonMail. Because the interface was a easy solution. But securemyemail didnt seem easier but seemed more secure.
usability_preview.2. Did either tool feel more secure or t: Both but securemyemail more. ProtonMail: Password protection. SecureMyEmail: Passphrase, and a signature that i am sending it.
usability_preview.3. What was the most frustrating part of: SecureMyEmail passphrase was being interchanged. There was two. One set initially and one regenerated later. Both were being applicable at different times.
usability_preview.4. What would stop you from using encryp: Normaly i dont send email. But if i need to send some confidential file i would send it through encrypted email. But i dont send mails or files that are confidential regularly.
usability_preview.5. How steep was the learning curve for: ProtonMail: Less learning element. SecureMyEmail: More learning element. ProtonMail would be easier to adopt for regular use.
usability_preview.6. If you could improve one thing in eac: ProtonMail: More secure options like secureMyEmail SecureMyEmail: Introduce web interface. and improve the compose mail.
usability_preview.7. Would you recommend any of these tool: If they work with confidential file in day to day life, then i would recommend them using SecureMyEmail. If they just want to continue a secure channel in day to day life, i would recommend ProtonMail.
usability_preview.8. In what personal or professional cont: If I send OTP, Financial transactions, top secret documents, question papers.

usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: Message send and recieve seeing. I was not understaing where will i confogure or find setting.
usability_preview.9. Did anything confuse or surprise you?: I am not sure, app setting and mail setting was same. I was not sure where would i search. when i found them , they were then gone.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: Mail security options should be clear , concise and should be displayed or differentiated from others.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: Regular mail sending. Seeing lock button seemed to set encryption. But didnt know if the text or attchment was encrypted or not. SO there should be a toggle to see the normal text and the encrypted text. I was not sure if they were going non encrypted because of UI issue.
usability_preview.19. Did anything confuse or surprise you: If encryption was being done. It didnt come up to me that expiration time could be set. If anyone wouldnt tell me about this, i would nt notice it.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in: There should be toggle to see the encrypted and normal text. And highlights of the encryption options available in the site.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: I was trying to understand which was sent by me and sent to me. I didnt understand which was encrypted. Which was the response to which mail i didnt quite get it.
usability_preview.29. Did anything confuse or surprise you: A mail thread didnt mention whose mail it was. The mail sent to me, i didnt see any expiration.
usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: Ui not so good but easy to navigate. I didnt like giving permission to everything. I dont want to see past mails here. I just want to see the future encrypted mails here.
usability_preview.9. Did anything confuse or surprise you?: The settings tab is named advanced.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: The settings tab should be renamed.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: I am sending encrypted mail, but when the user is using securemyemail., there is a psssowrd set option. but when i am sending to a normal gmail, then the password set is not accessible.
usability_preview.19. Did anything confuse or surprise you: The tooltip of the icon should clarify the user is a securemyemail user or not.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in: Clear indication of user mails state. The additional security parameter setting was not clear.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: Thread management was unclear to me. I was not able to figure out the encrypted or unencrypted mails at first. Then i figured it out. The lock should have a proper tooltip.
usability_preview.30. What would you improve or change in: Lock sign tooltip, encrypted tab should be at top and hightlighted properly.
usability_preview.1. Which of the two tools did you find e: SecureMyEmail. It was easily accessible. It was easy to identify encrypted message, and if the reciever will receive securely or not there is a option to see that.
usability_preview.2. Did either tool feel more secure or t: Neutral. I am not sure how these are working. I need to check thoroughly if someone can or cannot hack me. I am just seeing messages that this is secure. But this could be a hoax also.
usability_preview.4. What would stop you from using encryp: I have no such necessity. If i had any, i would consider if the receiver is able to decrypt it properly.
usability_preview.5. How steep was the learning curve for: ProtonMail: Very steep. SecureMyEmail: Very normal like gmail. Securemyemail would be easier to adopt.
usability_preview.6. If you could improve one thing in eac: Thread management for both. Expiration tab in protonMail, settings tabs should be straightforward. Securemyemail should have tooltips. Securemyemail redirects to a another page to set password. There should be highlights of encrypted or unencrypted message. Also the attachment should be encrypted too.
usability_preview.7. Would you recommend any of these tool: SecureMyEmail: For hard core user ProtonMail: For nice UI user.
usability_preview.8. In what personal or professional cont: When i share any password or contract.

usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: I was thinking if it would be different. It was normal account creation like any other website/gmail. The password setting was strong. It was easy for me. But it could be difficult for other users. The recaptcha used here was very easy to use. Solving the puzzle was easy.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: There were some username that already existed. WHen i created account in Gmail, that shows different combination of username possible. But there were no suggested username. It was not so much user friendly. Maybe it can be a difficult task to think of a different username. They wouldnt take the hassle.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: Apart from using Gmail, i was expecting something different. There was a option to set encryption for non protonmail user. There was a icon for password set.
usability_preview.19. Did anything confuse or surprise you: The expiration time. I think there was a threshold timeline which i couldn't change. I think there is a time limit. Some users may use more time for expiration.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in: Overall experience was okay. But the expiration date could have some flexibilities. The password length, for some it could be an irritating task. But i know the psycology behind the password. But it could be an issue for general users.
usability_preview.28. What were you thinking as you comple: I was expecting something different and unusual. When i received the emails it was no different. I think some may forget to add password when replying to the encrypted mail.
usability_preview.29. Did anything confuse or surprise you: I think those who have any knowledge about http or https lock color symbols will notice the lock color. For the general user, they wont recognize the difference. But prior to my knowledge, i could identify them.
usability_preview.30. What would you improve or change in: I think, a small label like if the mail is encrypted or not could be added at the side. Some people may miss the color difference.
usability_preview.8. What were you thinking as you complet: I was thinking/hopeful about the encryption process. It was not secured i think previously. I will sending personal/professional documents securely through this. I am hopeful about the whole process.
usability_preview.10. What would you improve or change in: Its fine. I need to install an authenticator. It should have a specific guideline to install the authenticator app.
usability_preview.18. What were you thinking as you comple: I was expecting this one would be more secure than the previous ones.
usability_preview.20. What would you improve or change in: There was a icon which lead me to set additional security. But i think, before i send the mail, they should give me suggestions weather i want to add additional security parameters or not. They should have suggestions for setting those.
usability_preview.2. Did either tool feel more secure or t: Both. I am setting a passowrd that psycologically convinced me to feel so.
usability_preview.3. What was the most frustrating part of: Locating the icons for setting/security passwords.
usability_preview.4. What would stop you from using encryp: Setting password for each of the mail.
usability_preview.5. How steep was the learning curve for: ProtonMail: Stnadalone app SecureMyEmail: had to integrate another software so steep learning curve.
usability_preview.6. If you could improve one thing in eac: The interfaces could be more directing and suggestion oriented.
usability_preview.7. Would you recommend any of these tool: Sure. For securing their communications. Like if my friend or colleage has to send mail periodically that is confidential, i would recommend using it.
usability_preview.8. In what personal or professional cont: For sending, term final questions, marks, certificates, LOR.

excerpt_preview.text: Normal mail sending, There are a lot of features/ Need time to check.

excerpt_preview.text: proton mail was more easier even though that a different service, theire user interface was good.encryption key was ssimple in proton mail. in secure my mail, for a general user it will be confussing. i couldnt understand
